core_mechanism: A conjugation identity that expresses a fixed iterate of a canonical structure-preserving anti-automorphism as an inner automorphism (conjugation by distinguished invertible/group-like elements), which forces that map to have finite order modulo inner automorphisms.
brief_reason: The abstract derives a Radford-type formula writing the fourth power of the antipode S as conjugation by group-like elements, implying S has finite order up to a trivial automorphism, and supplements it with a Tr(S^2) trace criterion for semisimplicity.
